title: Swarm Worker Evidence Result
description: Schema for files written by swarm workers to .agents/swarm/results/<task>.json. Workers spawned via /swarm or /crank emit one of these per task. The shape is intentionally permissive: historical results vary widely, and task-specific extension fields are allowed via additionalProperties.

task.description: Task identifier — usually an issue ID, epic-task slug, or short slug. Either 'task' or 'task_id' is required.

issue_id.description: Bead/issue identifier (used by /crank workers spawned per issue).

epic.description: Epic identifier (used by epic-level swarm rollups).

worker.description: Worker name (used by named-worker results that don't carry a task ID).

status.description: Outcome of the task. 'done' and 'pass' indicate full success; 'partial' indicates incomplete; 'failed'/'fail' indicates worker error; 'blocked' indicates upstream dependency missing.

type.description: Optional taxonomy used by /crank for routing. Most workers omit this.

files_changed.description: Repo-relative paths of files the worker created or modified.

files_created.description: Repo-relative paths of files the worker created (subset of files_changed when both present).

tests.description: Test outcome. Either a short status string or a structured object with details.

gate.description: Pre-push gate status (if the worker ran one).

evidence.description: Either a freeform string description of evidence, or a structured object containing required_checks (array) and checks (object map of check_name -> {verdict: PASS|FAIL|SKIP, ...}).

artifacts.description: Paths to artifacts produced (plans, reports, generated files).

notes.description: Freeform narrative explaining what was done, why, and any caveats.

summary.description: Short one-line summary of outcome (alternative to notes for terse results).
